name: r128_sweet_spot
description: Find the noise-free sweet spot on rank 128. LoRA+ ratio=16 caused noise — testing higher base LR without LoRA+ as a cleaner alternative. Target loss range 0.25–0.35. Also probing rank 256 since 102GB VRAM allows it.

experiments.description: LR=2e-4. Conservative 2× step up from baseline — noise-free descent toward sweet spot.
LR=3e-4. 3× baseline — landed at 0.41 on r64, should reach 0.25–0.35 on r128.
LR=5e-4. Aggressive but no LoRA+ B-matrix asymmetry — cleaner noise profile.
Curriculum only at baseline LR. Clean slow descent — reference for what curriculum contributes alone.
LR=3e-4 + curriculum. Speed of higher LR with coverage of curriculum — no LoRA+.
LR=3e-4 + curriculum + dropout=0.05. Full controlled stack without LoRA+.
LoRA+ ratio=4 (lr_B=4e-4). Much more conservative than ratio=16 — tests if noise came from ratio not the technique.
Rank 256 at baseline LR. 102GB VRAM makes this viable — does more capacity keep helping?
Rank 256 + LR=3e-4. Best rank + best LR candidate combined.
